steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: 15% of the initial voltage: $0.15 \times 16 = 2.4$ volts. This is the target level.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The graph shows exponential decay of the form $V(t) = 16e^{-t/\tau}$. Setting $V = 2.4$: $e^{-t/\tau} = 0.15$, so $t = -\tau \ln(0.15) \approx 1.897\tau$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: From the graph, the time constant $\tau$ can be read as the time for the voltage to fall to $16/e \approx 5.9$ volts. Reading the graph gives $\tau \approx 0.90$ seconds, so $t \approx 1.897 \times 0.90 \approx 1.71$ seconds.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • 0.43 s: Voltage at 0.43 s is well above 2.4 V (roughly 10.4 V). Eliminated.
  • 0.85 s: Voltage is still above 2.4 V at this time (roughly 5.9 V). Eliminated.
  • 1.71 s: Matches the computed time for 15% of initial voltage. C is correct.
  • 3.41 s: Voltage has decayed far below 2.4 V by this time. Eliminated.
